accepted.claim: Industry compute build is roughly 10-15 GW this year, growing ~3x to 30-40 GW next year, ~100 GW in 2028, ~300 GW in 2029, at ~$10-15B per gigawatt.
accepted.speaker: Dario Amodei
accepted.comparison_note: Implies industry-wide capex of $100-225B in 2025 from gigawatt build, vs ledger total 2025 capex of $330B.

triangulation.derivation: Source says industry builds 10-15 GW in 2025 at $10-15B/GW. Implied capex = 10-15 GW × $10-15B = $100B to $225B. Ledger 2025 total capex = $330B (mag7 $250B + neoclouds $45B + sovereign $20B + enterprise $15B). Source figure is lower because gigawatts measures power capacity, not total capex (which includes non-power-build spend). Delta: -$105B to -$230B.

accepted.speaker: Dario Amodei
accepted.comparison_note: Forward projection implies ~10x ledger's 2025 total capex ($330B) by 2029.

triangulation.derivation: Source: 100 GW in 2028 × ~$12.5B = $1.25T; 300 GW in 2029 × ~$12.5B = $3.75T. Ledger 2025 total = $330B. Implied 2028 capex is ~3.8x 2025 level; 2029 is ~11x. Confirms aggressive 3x/yr industry trajectory but well above ledger's current 22.7% YoY growth pace.

accepted.dedup_note: Forward projection implies ~10x ledger's 2025 total capex ($330B) by 2029.

accepted.claim: Enterprise AI has surged from $1.7B to $37B since 2023, now capturing 6% of the global SaaS market.
accepted.speaker: Menlo Ventures
accepted.comparison_note: Menlo's $37B Enterprise GenAI spend covers infra + apps + foundation models; maps roughly to Enterprise + SME buyer segments plus part of capex.

triangulation.derivation: Menlo says Enterprise GenAI = $37B (apps $19B + infra $18B). Our model: Enterprise buyer ($10.1B) + SME buyer ($3.8B) = $13.9B in customer revenue. Adding enterprise direct capex ($15B) gets $28.9B. Gap to $37B (~$8B) likely reflects Menlo's inclusion of foundation model API spend routed through enterprises that we attribute to providers via Hyperscaler/API channels. Implied: enterprise+SME segment may be undercounted by ~$5-8B, OR Menlo includes scope our ledger excludes (training infra, orchestration tools).

accepted.source_excerpt:
nd side tells a different story: Our latest market data shows broad adoption, real revenue, and productivity gains at scale, signaling a boom versus a bubble. 
Enterprise AI has surged from $1.7B to $**37**B since 2023, now capturing 6% of the global SaaS market and growing faster than any software category in history.
For Menlo’s third annual State of Generative AI in the Enterprise report, we surveyed
accepted.dedup_note: Menlo's $37B Enterprise GenAI spend covers infra + apps + foundation models; maps roughly to Enterprise + SME buyer segments plus part of capex.

accepted.claim: AI Applications captured $19 billion in 2025, more than half of all generative AI spending.
accepted.speaker: Menlo Ventures
accepted.comparison_note: Maps to AI Native Apps + Trad. SaaS channels in our model.

triangulation.derivation: Menlo: AI applications = $19B in 2025. Our model: AI Native Apps ($0.7B) + Trad. SaaS ($2.6B) = $3.3B. Even adding Model Subs ($7.1B, since copilots like ChatGPT Enterprise are subs), gets $10.4B. Gap to $19B is ~$8.6B — suggests our AI Native Apps + Trad. SaaS channels significantly undercount the application layer, OR Menlo's $19B includes spend on copilots/subs we route through Model Subs channel.

accepted.claim: Anthropic now earns 40% of enterprise LLM spend, up from 24% last year and 12% in 2023.
accepted.speaker: Menlo Ventures
accepted.comparison_note: Menlo enterprise LLM share for Anthropic at 40% triangulates our provider revenue split.

triangulation.derivation: Menlo: Anthropic 40%, OpenAI 27%, Google 21% of enterprise LLM spend. In our ledger across 2025 provider revenue, Anthropic $7.3B / OpenAI $13.7B / Google $2.9B = total $23.9B. Anthropic share = 7.3/23.9 = 31%; OpenAI = 57%; Google = 12%. Menlo's enterprise-only view shows Anthropic ahead, ours (all-segment) shows OpenAI dominant. Reconcilable: OpenAI's $13.7B is heavily Consumer/SME via subs; in pure enterprise API, Anthropic likely leads. Suggests our Anthropic enterprise allocation is consistent but our channel routing should weight Anthropic Model API higher.

vative. When we account for “shadow AI adoption”—employees using personal credit cards for tools like ChatGPT Plus, where roughly 27% of usage is work-related6—PLG-driven tools may represent close to **40**% of application AI spend.

accepted.dedup_note: Enterprise-only share; not directly mappable since our provider revenue is all-segment.

The application layer captured $19 billion in 2025, more than half of all generative AI spending. This spend segments into three categories:

Vertical AI ($3.5 billion), targeting specific industries like healthcare or finance; and

Horizontal AI ($8.4 billion), in
accepted.dedup_note: New sub-segment of application layer not currently broken out in ledger.

accepted.claim: Coding is the largest departmental AI category at $4.0 billion (55% of departmental AI spend), up from $550M in 2024.
accepted.speaker: Menlo Ventures
accepted.comparison_note: Coding tools market triangulates against our coding-focused app entities.

triangulation.derivation: Menlo: AI coding tools = $4.0B in 2025. In our ledger summing 2025 ARR for coding apps: Cursor $1.0B + GitHub Copilot $1.65B + Cognition $0.155B + Replit $0.252B + Lovable $0.4B = $3.46B. Plus Anthropic Claude Code direct (not separately tracked) likely $0.3-0.5B. Total ~$3.8-4.0B aligns with Menlo's $4.0B. Strengthens our coding app ARRs.

across all functions.

Departmental AI: Coding Is Generative AI’s First “Killer Use Case”

Departmental AI spending hit $7.3 billion in 2025, up 4.1x year over year. Coding is the clear standout at $**4.0** billion (55% of departmental AI spend), making it the largest category across the entire application layer; the rest spans IT (10%), marketing (9%), customer success (9%), design (7%), and HR (5%).

triangulation.derivation: Menlo: scribe market = $600M in 2025, with Abridge + Ambience as new unicorns alongside leader Nuance DAX. If Nuance DAX has ~50% share = $300M, and Abridge + Ambience split ~$200M (= $100M each), plus tail $100M. This implies Abridge ARR ~$100M in 2025, vs our ledger's $50M. Delta: +$50M / +100%. Suggests our abridge.2025.arr=0.05 [low] is undercounted by roughly 2x.

accepted.dedup_note: Scribe market triangulates against Abridge ARR which is currently low-confidence.

accepted.claim: Horizontal AI is $8.4 billion, 5.3x YoY. Copilots dominate at 86% share ($7.2B); Agent platforms 10% ($750M); personal productivity 5% ($450M).

accepted.comparison_note: Copilot revenue triangulates our Model Subs channel.

triangulation.derivation: Menlo: copilot spend = $7.2B in 2025 (ChatGPT Enterprise, Claude for Work, MS Copilot). In our ledger Model Subs channel = $7.1B. These are essentially the same construct. Match within 2%. Strongly corroborates our Model Subs sizing.

Outside of centralized procurement channels, AI solutions are increasingly finding their initial “land” in the enterprise through individual users rather than the enterprise executive. We found that **27**% of all AI application spend comes through product-led growth (PLG) motions, nearly 4x the rate in traditional software (7%).

accepted.dedup_note: GTM motion split; not mapped to ledger.

accepted.claim: Startups captured 63% of AI app market share, up from 36% last year.
accepted.speaker: Menlo Ventures
accepted.comparison_note: Startup vs incumbent split; informational.
accepted.source_excerpt:
ants Gain Ground in AI Apps

At the AI application layer, startups have pulled decisively ahead. This year, according to our data, they captured nearly $2 in revenue for every $1 earned by incumbents—**63**% of the market, up from 36% last year7 when enterprises still held the lead.

On paper, this shouldn’t be happening. Incumbents have entrenched distribution, data moats, deep enterprise relationships
accepted.dedup_note: Startup vs incumbent split; informational.

ce, where reliability and deep integrations outweigh speed.
The story changes as we move down the stack. At the infrastructure layer, the picture is more mixed. According to our data, incumbents hold **56**% of the market as many AI app builders continue building on the data platforms they’ve trusted for years. Although new AI-native infrastructure companies like Temporal, Supabase, Neon*, and Pinecone*
accepted.dedup_note: Market structure context.

declined.claim: Anthropic is maybe at a $22 billion run rate, and the revenue does tie to the conclusions about enterprise share shift.
The founder of Grok (Jonathan) is going to make about $950 million after double taxation from the Nvidia acquisition deal.
Chamath reportedly made $950 million from the Grok deal, though he claimed on Twitter he made much more.
Anthropic added another few billion to revenue in January 2026, suggesting current ARR run-rate materially above $10B exit-2025.
Country of geniuses in a datacenter likely arrives 2026-2027, with Dario at ~90% confidence by 2035 and ~50% confidence in 1-3 years.
Coding models currently provide ~15-20% total factor productivity speedup, up from ~5% six months ago.
Stylized example: model trained for $1B last year produces $4B revenue and costs $1B to inference, ~75% gross margins.
Buying $1T/year of compute starting end of 2027 for 5 years would equal $5T of compute commitment.
Cursor reached $200 million in revenue before hiring a single enterprise sales rep.
Anthropic monthly revenue run rate of $7B/month in Q1 2026 exit.
Mistral's H1 2026 revenue ran at $250M, doubling from H2 2025 levels.

parked.claim: Foundation model APIs captured $12.5 billion in 2025.
Anthropic posted a $7B/month revenue run rate exiting Q1 2026, implying an exit ARR of $84B annualized.
